assertions.text: Flags that --password leaks the secret via ps and shell history; recommends --password-file or stdin
Flags that -F (capital) is non-standard for force — convention is -f lowercase
Flags that -V (capital) is non-standard for version — convention is --version (and -V often means verbose)
Flags that --dry should be --dry-run to match convention
Notes that --output human is not a stable scriptable format — recommends --json (and ideally --plain)
Notes the destructive scope (deletes pods, rotates secrets) warrants severe-action confirmation (typing the resource name or --confirm=name) beyond a -y/--yes toggle
Cites at least three distinct clig.dev anchor URLs
Notes -N for namespace is unusual capitalization — short flags are conventionally lowercase

prompt: Reviewing this PR for our `deployctl` tool — a Cobra-based CLI that SREs use to push services to staging and prod. Tell me what's wrong with the flag layout. Be specific and cite where the rule comes from. (See evals.json for the code block.) This thing deletes pods and rotates secrets. Be honest.
